user.title: Legrand Digital Audio
user.description:
Choose which Legrand device to add. Found {au7000_count} distribution module(s) (AU7000) and {au7001_count} streaming module(s) (AU7001) on your network.

**AU7000** controls whole-house zones (power, volume, sources).

**AU7001** controls streaming (Pandora browse, Music Assistant). Requires bind in the Digital Audio app (solid white LED on the module).

manual.title: AU7000 — manual setup
manual.description: Enter the IP address of your **distribution module (AU7000)**. This is the unit that drives your speaker zones. Port **2112** is used on all known hardware.

manual_au7001.title: AU7001 — manual setup
manual_au7001.description:
Enter the IP address of your **streaming module (AU7001)**. The integration will locate it via UPnP/SSDP on that address.

Make sure the module is bound in the Digital Audio app first (bind button + **solid white LED**).

discovery_confirm.title: Add distribution module (AU7000)
discovery_confirm.description:
Add the Legrand distribution module at **{host}**?

This creates media player entities for each whole-house zone (power, volume, source selection).

ssdp_confirm.title: Add streaming module (AU7001)
ssdp_confirm.description:
Add **{name}** as a streaming media player?

**Before using streaming:**
1. Open the Digital Audio app → Settings → **Bind Digital Source** and press the bind button on the AU7001 until the LED is **solid white**.
2. Sign in to Pandora/Spotify in the app if you want those services.

**Music Assistant:** After setup, go to Music Assistant → Settings → Player providers → enable **Home Assistant**, then add this entity as a player. The AU7001 must stay bound (Active) for playback to work.

recommend_companion.title: Add companion device?
recommend_companion.description:
We also found **{companion_label}** on your network.

Most installs use **both** modules: the AU7000 for zone control and the AU7001 for streaming. Add the companion device now?

error.cannot_connect: Could not connect to the AU7000 on port 2112. Check the IP address and that the module is powered on.
error.cannot_connect_au7001: Could not find an AU7001 at that IP. Make sure it is powered on and bound (solid white LED).
